description: Deterministic profiling receipt contract for scheduler and resource-pressure hot paths. The receipts identify bounded scenarios, static source evidence, expected hot-path attribution, memory observations, operator interpretation, and RCH-routed commands for refreshing real benchmark evidence.

scenario_catalog.title: Scheduler spawn storm and local/global queue pressure
scenario_catalog.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_scheduler_spawn_storm CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_BENCH_DEBUG=0 cargo bench -p asupersync --bench methodology_baselines --features test-internals -- methodology/task_spawn --noplot
scenario_catalog.rch_refresh_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_scheduler_spawn_storm CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_BENCH_DEBUG=0 cargo bench -p asupersync --bench methodology_baselines --features test-internals -- methodology/task_spawn --noplot

top_hot_paths.expected_pressure: global-ready injection lock and wake coordination
owner-local queue push/pop locality under batch spawn
idle-worker permit recheck and targeted wakeup cost

memory_observations.interpretation: Contract fixture stays bounded; real Criterion artifacts are generated only by the RCH refresh command.
Large-host profiling refresh should run on a remote worker with enough memory for debug-stripped Criterion artifacts.

operator_interpretation.recommended_action: Refresh the RCH benchmark before making throughput or regression-closure claims; use this receipt to route analysis to scheduler queue and wake paths.
operator_interpretation.claim_boundary: This row is not a fresh benchmark result.

scenario_catalog.title: Cancellation drain and obligation cleanup pressure
scenario_catalog.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_obligation_cleanup CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_BENCH_DEBUG=0 cargo bench -p asupersync --bench methodology_baselines --features test-internals -- methodology/task_cancellation --noplot
scenario_catalog.rch_refresh_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_obligation_cleanup CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_BENCH_DEBUG=0 cargo bench -p asupersync --bench methodology_baselines --features test-internals -- methodology/task_cancellation --noplot

top_hot_paths.expected_pressure: region cancellation fan-out and task collection
orphan obligation scan and terminal-state accounting

memory_observations.interpretation: The contract records expected attribution without retaining full benchmark samples.
Cancellation and obligation refresh lanes should remain below broad test-frontier memory.

operator_interpretation.recommended_action: If drain or shutdown latency is suspected, refresh this lane and compare top paths before changing cancellation policy.
operator_interpretation.claim_boundary: This row does not prove no obligation leaks; it points at profiling surfaces for cleanup cost.

scenario_catalog.title: Proof-lane parsing and report generation pressure
scenario_catalog.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_proof_lane_report CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 bash scripts/run_swarm_pressure_preflight_report_e2e.sh --run-id profile-proof-lane-report
scenario_catalog.rch_refresh_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_proof_lane_report CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 bash scripts/run_swarm_pressure_preflight_report_e2e.sh --run-id profile-proof-lane-report

top_hot_paths.expected_pressure: proof-lane manifest projection and resource envelope analysis
proof-status row classification and dirty-frontier reasons

memory_observations.interpretation: Fixture rows are bounded and deterministic; the helper does not invoke Cargo or RCH by itself.
Report refresh is artifact-contract sized, not a broad workspace proof.

operator_interpretation.recommended_action: Use this receipt when report-generation overhead or proof-lane parsing churn is suspected; do not cite it as broad workspace health.
operator_interpretation.claim_boundary: This row is report-contract attribution only.

scenario_catalog.title: Dirty-tree ownership and reservation correlation pressure
scenario_catalog.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_dirty_tree_correlation CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 bash scripts/run_dirty_tree_ownership_receipt_e2e.sh --run-id profile-dirty-tree-correlation
scenario_catalog.rch_refresh_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_profile_dirty_tree_correlation CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 bash scripts/run_dirty_tree_ownership_receipt_e2e.sh --run-id profile-dirty-tree-correlation

top_hot_paths.expected_pressure: git status parsing and dirty path ownership classification
reservation glob matching and implementation conflict detection
golden drift detection for peer dirty-tree handoff rows

memory_observations.interpretation: Dirty-tree correlation fixtures stay bounded even when they model many active reservations.
Ownership receipt refresh is artifact-contract sized; broad git history analysis is outside this lane.

operator_interpretation.recommended_action: Refresh this lane when dirty-tree ownership checks dominate operator handoff time or conflict explanations look stale.
operator_interpretation.claim_boundary: This row does not prove reservation freshness or active peer liveness.
